differential_diagnoses: Systemic fungal infection (e.g., Histoplasmosis or Cryptococcosis)
next_steps: Perform bronchoalveolar lavage with cell count, Gram stain, fungal/bacterial/viral cultures, and microscopy for Strongyloides larvae
Obtain skin biopsy of the purpuric rash for histopathology and tissue culture
Check serum Strongyloides antibody and repeat stool studies for ova and parasites
Evaluate for adrenal insufficiency given the shock and history of chronic steroid use
Review peripheral blood smear for schistocytes or atypical cells
Perform abdominal CT scan with contrast to evaluate for bowel perforation or ischemic colitis
Start empiric treatment with ivermectin for suspected Strongyloides hyperinfection
